description: Layer 3 entity registry establishing cross-layer linkage between compatibility rule candidates (CompatibilityLayer) and the authoritative Layer 1 canonical entity registry (RC2). Records all resolved and unresolved entity discoveries from the compatibility candidate scan. Serves as the entity bridge for Phase 4 rule production.

resolved_entities.resolution_pass: pass 2 — 'NIC Firmware' found verbatim in FW-005.aliases
pass 4 — token 'SIEM' in raw string matches MGT-008 canonical_name case-insensitively
resolved_entities.resolution_scope_caveat: Platform concept only. Third-party connector software and ACME Partner Program certification are not resolved.

cross_layer_link.layer3_entity_name: SIEM platform reference in 'third-party SIEM connectors'

unresolved_entities.unresolved_reason: Synthetic enterprise OS product not registered in RC2. No canonical name, alias, or normalized name match across all 54 RC2 entities.

No RC2 entity with canonical name or alias 'System Firmware'. Proven distinct from FW-001 (BIOS) by co-occurrence evidence in RCAND-000363 where both System BIOS and System Firmware appear in the same rule.

suggested_registration.alternative: Add as alias to FW-002 (UEFI) if domain expert confirms equivalence — requires explicit confirmation before alias addition

unresolved_entities.data_quality_flag: RCAND-000364 and RCAND-000374 mislabel 'System Firmware' with component_type='bios' — extractor error

unresolved_entities.unresolved_reason: RC2 driver registry models individual driver types only. No bundle/distribution entity exists. 'Driver Pack' is a multi-driver aggregate distribution package with no RC2 equivalent.
No RC2 entity. Hardware-context-specific driver bundle variant. Used in EdgeStation-specific and OS compatibility contexts alongside but distinct from 'Driver Pack'.

suggested_registration.alternative: Evaluate as alias of DRV-009 (Driver Pack) once that entity is registered

unresolved_entities.unresolved_reason: No RC2 entity has 'Security Agent' as canonical name or alias. SEC-004 (EDR Agent) is closest semantic match but has no aliases; explicit alias match is required for resolution.

suggested_registration.primary_recommendation: Add 'Security Agent' as alias to SEC-004 (EDR Agent) after domain expert confirmation
suggested_registration.cve_evidence: CVE SEC-2026-003 titled 'Endpoint Agent Token Exposure' supports EDR Agent classification

unresolved_entities.unresolved_reason: MGT-010 (Endpoint Agent) is the closest RC2 entity but aliases are 'Client Agent' and 'Management Agent' only — the combined form 'Endpoint Management Agent' is absent. RCAND-000395 confirms 'Endpoint Mgmt Agent' and 'Endpoint Management Agent' refer to the same product.

suggested_registration.primary_recommendation: Add 'Endpoint Management Agent' and 'Endpoint Mgmt Agent' as aliases to MGT-010 (Endpoint Agent). No new entity registration required.
suggested_registration.confidence: high — source context strongly confirms equivalence

unresolved_entities.unresolved_reason: Hardware device family. RC2 Layer 1 registry covers software entities only. Hardware platforms are structurally out of scope.

suggested_registration.primary_recommendation: No Layer 1 action required. Document as hardware platform scope reference. A separate hardware device registry would be required if hardware entity modeling is needed.

unresolved_entities.unresolved_reason: Hardware device family. Same out-of-scope classification as 'ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series'.

suggested_registration.primary_recommendation: No Layer 1 action required. Document as hardware platform scope reference.

data_quality_findings.description: RCAND-000364 and RCAND-000374 assign component_type='bios' to 'System Firmware'. Version evidence (8.x.x for System Firmware vs. 6.x.x for BIOS) and RCAND-000363 co-occurrence proof confirm these are distinct components. The mislabeling is an extractor error.
data_quality_findings.recommended_fix: Correct component_type to 'firmware' for 'System Firmware' records in normalized_rule_candidates.json, or document the mislabeling in the entity resolution record for Phase 4.

data_quality_findings.description: Multiple candidates have component_name=null in raw metadata; the normalized layer fills component_name with the type string ('bios', 'firmware', 'driver'). These are valid but derived names, not original source text.

data_quality_findings.issue: Readiness process artifacts extracted as component names
data_quality_findings.description: 'Reboot Cycle' (RCAND-000398) and 'Step 1' (RCAND-000401) appear in requirements[].component_name with component_type='readiness'. These are procedural references incorrectly structured as component requirements by the extractor. They were excluded from entity resolution.

recommended_rc2_changes.summary: 6 changes to RC2 registry required to achieve full resolution coverage. 2 are alias additions requiring minimal change; 4 require new entity registrations.

priority_order.rationale: Source text in RCAND-000395 explicitly equates both forms. No domain expert confirmation needed.
Proven distinct from BIOS by RCAND-000363 co-occurrence. Most referenced unresolved entity in the candidate set (13 candidates).
Second most referenced unresolved entity (10 candidates). Required for OS-level compatibility rules.
Most referenced entity in the candidate set overall (10 candidates). All OS-level rules depend on this entity.
8 candidates reference Security Agent. Priority lower because resolution is conditional on domain expert confirmation of SEC-004 equivalence.
4 candidates. May be resolved as alias of DRV-009 once that entity exists.
